feat: Event Subscription System — In-Process Event Bus (#11)

* feat: Event Subscription System (OtelEvents.Subscriptions) (#11)

Implement event subscription system that dispatches OTEL log events
to registered handlers via a background channel, never blocking the
OTEL pipeline.

New package: OtelEvents.Subscriptions

Components:
- OtelEventsSubscriptionProcessor: BaseProcessor<LogRecord> that
  snapshots matching events and writes to a bounded Channel
- OtelEventContext: Immutable LogRecord snapshot with GetAttribute<T>
- IOtelEventHandler: Interface for DI-resolved handler classes
- OtelEventsSubscriptionBuilder: Fluent API for lambda + DI handlers
- OtelEventsSubscriptionDispatcher: BackgroundService dispatch loop
- OtelEventsSubscriptionOptions: Channel capacity + backpressure config
- SubscriptionMetrics: Self-telemetry counters

Features:
- Subscribe by exact event name or wildcard (trailing *)
- Wildcard matching uses longest-prefix-first (reuses severity filter pattern)
- Lambda handlers via .On(pattern, handler)
- DI-resolved handlers via .AddHandler<T>(pattern)
- Background dispatch via System.Threading.Channels (non-blocking)
- Handler errors caught, metered, and swallowed (never crash the app)
- Self-telemetry: events_dispatched, handler_errors, channel_full
- Configurable channel capacity (default 1024) and BoundedChannelFullMode

Tests: 32 tests covering lambda/DI handlers, wildcard matching,
channel backpressure, error isolation, self-telemetry, concurrent
dispatch safety, and disposal/shutdown.

* fix: address review findings — public OtelEventContext, ILogger in dispatcher, TryAddTransient

- OtelEventContext constructor now public (consumers can unit test IOtelEventHandler)
- Dispatcher logs handler exceptions at Warning level (not silently swallowed)
- TryAddTransient prevents duplicate DI registrations
- Security: handler timeout and Channel itemDropped callback tracked as follow-ups

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: distinguish shutdown from handler timeout in dispatcher

Shutdown cancellation no longer inflates handler_timeouts counter.
Ordered catch: stoppingToken first (shutdown), then cancellationToken (timeout).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: slot count decrement + rate card + QR fill

Slot count fix (root cause):
- getAllApproved() was binding :now as a PHP parameter inside a correlated
  subquery — some MySQL/PDO versions evaluate this as NULL silently,
  making COUNT() always return 0 and available_slots = total_slots always.
  Fixed by using MySQL NOW() directly in the SQL (no binding needed).
- Added GREATEST(0, ...) so available_slots never goes negative.

fileserver: migrate pingcap/tidb artifact downloads to OCI registry (#4274)

Removes all `fileserver.pingcap.net` references from `pingcap/tidb`
pipelines and jobs, replacing binary artifact fetching with OCI pulls
via `hub-zot.pingcap.net/mirrors/hub`.

## Core pattern change

**Before:**
```groovy
environment { FILE_SERVER_URL = 'http://fileserver.pingcap.net' }
// ...
component.fetchAndExtractArtifact(FILE_SERVER_URL, 'tikv', REFS.base_ref, ...)
component.fetchAndExtractArtifact(FILE_SERVER_URL, 'pd',   REFS.base_ref, ...)
```

**After:**
```groovy
environment { OCI_ARTIFACT_HOST = 'hub-zot.pingcap.net/mirrors/hub' }
// ...
final OCI_TAG_PD   = component.computeArtifactOciTagFromPR('pd',   REFS.base_ref, ...)
final OCI_TAG_TIKV = component.computeArtifactOciTagFromPR('tikv', REFS.base_ref, ...)
// ...
container("utils") {
    dir("bin") {
        retry(3) {
            sh label: 'download tidb components', script: """
                ${WORKSPACE}/scripts/artifacts/download_pingcap_oci_artifact.sh \
                    --pd=${OCI_TAG_PD} --tikv=${OCI_TAG_TIKV}
            """
        }
    }
}
```

## Changes by category

- **~60 integration/check2 test pipelines** (release-6.x →
release-9.0-beta, latest): replaced `fetchAndExtractArtifact` for
tikv/pd with OCI download; added `OCI_TAG_*` declarations; added `utils`
sidecar container to pod templates where missing
- **~13 build pipelines** (`pull_build`, `ghpr_build`): removed
fileserver artifact upload steps and CI-flag curl uploads — builds no
longer push to fileserver
- **`periodics_integration_test.groovy`**: rewrote to use OCI download,
removed sha1-based fileserver tracking
- **Tiflash integration tests**: added `OCI_TAG_TIFLASH`; replaced
tiflash binary and vector-search test data downloads (now pulled from
`ann-benchmarks.com`)
- **Sqllogic pod templates**: init containers now use `oras pull` from
OCI registry instead of `wget` from fileserver
- **`pull_br_integration_test_next_gen/download_tools.sh`**: localstack
binary now fetched from GitHub Releases; removed deprecated
`tikv-importer` download helper
- **`release-6.x/ghpr_unit_test.groovy`**: codecov binary now downloaded
from `uploader.codecov.io`; fileserver-based `junitUrl` cleared
- **`prow-jobs/pingcap/tidb/latest-periodics.yaml`**: updated stale
commented-out codecov URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(mc): fix all-solid phantom surfaces + re-mesh on neighbour load

Two fixes:

1. compute_surface_cache all-solid detection (chunk_loader.rs)
   When the topmost solid voxel is the very top of the chunk AND there are
   no air voxels anywhere in the column, the terrain continues above this
   chunk (e.g. the lower Y layer under a cliff or near sea-level terrain).
   Previously surface_y = max_v.y - 0.5 caused smooth MC to draw a phantom
   horizontal plane at the chunk top — the green cloud visible inside
   cliffs and on near-sea-level terrain.
   Fix: set surface_y = max_v.y + 0.5 → density always > 0 → no phantom.

2. Neighbour dirty-marking on load (chunk_streaming.rs)
   When chunk (x,y,z) finishes loading it becomes the +X neighbour of
   (x-1,y,z) and the +Z neighbour of (x,y,z-1).  Those chunks used the
   clamping fallback at mesh time (neighbour was absent).  Mark them dirty
   immediately so they re-mesh with the exact neighbour surface_cache on
   the next frame, eliminating the residual every-Nth-chunk seams.

Bump: v0.1.71 → v0.1.72

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 3, 4, 5: API routes, pages, and styling

Phase 3 - API Routes:
- POST /api/session - Create/join wedding sessions
- GET /api/session - Fetch session with photos
- POST /api/photos - Upload photo metadata
- GET /api/photos - Fetch photos by session
- POST /api/upload-token - Generate upload tokens

Phase 4 - Frontend Pages:
- (auth)/join - Enter wedding code to join
- (gallery)/page - Masonry gallery with real-time refresh
- (gallery)/upload - Camera/gallery upload with preview

Phase 5 - Styling & Mobile:
- Responsive design (mobile-first)
- Dark mode toggle with persistent state
- Masonry layout for photo gallery
- Smooth animations and transitions
- Mobile camera capture support
- Gradient backgrounds and hover effects

Features:
- Auto-refresh gallery every 3 seconds
- Camera + gallery file upload options
- Photo metadata with guest name and timestamp
- Error handling and loading states

- Dark/light theme support

docs: add agent WIP tracking & crash recovery instructions (#1383)

* docs: add mandatory issue labeling instructions for agents

Add a clear 'Issue Pickup & Labeling' section that requires agents to:
- Apply 'claimed:copilot-cli' label when picking up an issue
- Apply 'in-progress' label when starting work
- Remove 'in-progress' when PR is opened or work is done
- Never start coding without labeling first

Also updated the Development Workflow numbered list to reference
the new labeling step.

- Create WIP branch + draft PR early
- Commit early and often with state in commit messages
- Post plan/progress comments on the PR
- Tag PRs with machine ID, PID, session ID for stale detection
- Record worktree status for recovery context
- Recovery procedure for a new agent picking up crashed work
